prompt: Explain why recent agentic RL work treats stability as a first-class problem, and cite concrete failure modes or stabilization mechanisms.
reference_answer: Recent agentic RL work treats stability as a first-class problem because long-horizon training can collapse even when surface metrics look healthy. RAGEN-2 highlights template collapse and input-agnostic reasoning hidden from entropy, while ARLArena emphasizes training collapse in longer interactive settings and proposes SAMPO as a stabilization method. RAGEN also helps frame multi-turn reasoning as trajectory-level RL rather than isolated prompting.

documents.title: RAGEN overview digest
documents.content: RAGEN frames interactive reasoning as trajectory-level multi-turn reinforcement learning in stochastic environments. The project treats agent reasoning as a sequential control problem instead of isolated single-turn prompting, which makes long-horizon stability and credit assignment important.

documents.title: RAGEN-2 stability digest
documents.content: RAGEN-2 says recent multi-turn agentic RL treats stability as a first-class problem because long-horizon training can collapse. It identifies template collapse, where trajectories look diverse by entropy but become input-agnostic, and it proposes mutual information proxies plus SNR-aware filtering to diagnose and mitigate the problem.

documents.title: ARLArena digest
documents.content: ARLArena says agentic RL often leads to training collapse in larger environments and longer interaction horizons. It builds a standardized testbed, decomposes instability sources, and proposes SAMPO as a stable agentic policy optimization method.

required_claims.text: Recent agentic RL work treats stability as a first-class issue because long-horizon training can collapse.
Template collapse is a concrete failure mode in which reasoning becomes input-agnostic even when entropy looks stable.
Stabilization mechanisms include mutual information diagnostics, SNR-aware filtering, and SAMPO.
